user.title: What do you want to amortize?

direct.title: Set up direct use
direct.description:
Calculates the savings from measured self-consumption (production − export).

**Required:**
- **kWh production** from the inverter (one or more sensors)
- **kWh export** at the grid connection point (netted)
- **input_number** helpers for purchase cost and electricity price

**Important for correct values:**
- Use only **cumulative kWh** meters (state_class total / total_increasing), not watt sensors. If you only have watts, build a kWh sensor first via a Riemann integral helper.
- Export must be the **netted** value at the grid connection point (not at the device, not per phase).
- Multiple producers (e.g. balcony + large PV on the same meter) can be selected together – they are summed.
- Counting starts at zero. For an already running system, add a one-time starting credit below.

data.name: Name
data.production_sensor: PV production (one or more kWh sensors)
data.initial_auto: Calculate starting credit automatically from current meter readings

data_description.name: Free to choose (e.g. 'Balcony plant'). Determines the names of the created sensors.
data_description.production_sensor: Cumulative kWh production meters. Multiple allowed (balcony + PV) – they are summed. Not watt sensors.
data_description.to_grid_sensor: Cumulative kWh export at the grid connection point (netted). On a smart meter, the export register, not a net/watt value.
data_description.investment_entity: input_number/sensor with the purchase cost in €.
data_description.price_entity: input_number/sensor with the grid price in €/kWh (e.g. a Tibber sensor).
data_description.feed_in_entity: input_number/sensor with the tariff in €/kWh. Empty = 0 (default for balcony plants).
data_description.initial_auto: Self-consumption = current production − current export; valued with the current input_number prices. Only if all sensors share the same history (not a fresh helper).
data_description.initial_self_kwh: Manual mode (auto off): previously self-consumed kWh. Multiplied by the average grid price.
data_description.initial_export_kwh: Manual mode: previously exported kWh. Multiplied by the average feed-in tariff.
data_description.initial_price: Optional. Empty/0 = current price from the price input_number.
data_description.initial_feed_in: Optional. Empty/0 = current tariff from the input_number (or 0).
data_description.initial_savings: Optional. Added on top of the calculated starting credit.

storage.title: Set up storage
storage.description:
Separate amortization for the storage, independent of the panels.
Savings = discharge × grid price − charge × feed-in tariff (forgone export; deducted only if tariff > 0). Losses are implicit in the discharge figure.

**Required:**
- **kWh charge** and **kWh discharge** of the battery (storage/hybrid inverter)
- **input_number** for the storage purchase cost (separate from the panels)

**Important for correct values:**
- Use only **cumulative kWh** meters, not watt sensors.
- Works equally for AC- and DC-coupled storage – only charge/discharge energy matters.
- Assumption: the storage charges from PV surplus. Grid charging (arbitrage) is not modeled here.
- The roundtrip efficiency is informational only and does NOT enter the € calculation.

data.name: Name
data.initial_auto: Calculate starting credit automatically from current meter readings

data_description.name: Free to choose (e.g. 'Storage'). Determines the names of the created sensors.
data_description.charge_sensor: Cumulative kWh battery charge meter (often 'battery charge energy'). Not a watt sensor.
data_description.discharge_sensor: Cumulative kWh battery discharge meter (often 'battery discharge energy'). Not a watt sensor.
data_description.roundtrip_entity: Does your storage already provide efficiency? Select it here. Empty = the integration computes η = discharge/charge (needs several full cycles to settle).
data_description.investment_entity: input_number/sensor with the storage cost in €, separate from the panels.
data_description.price_entity: input_number/sensor with the grid price in €/kWh.
data_description.feed_in_entity: input_number/sensor with the tariff in €/kWh. Empty = 0 (then no deduction for forgone export).
data_description.initial_auto: Takes discharge/charge from current readings; valued with the input_number prices. Only if the meters share real, matching history.
data_description.initial_discharge_kwh: Manual mode: previously discharged kWh × average grid price.
data_description.initial_charge_kwh: Manual mode: previously charged kWh × average feed-in tariff (deduction).
data_description.initial_price: Optional. Empty/0 = current price from the price input_number.
data_description.initial_feed_in: Optional. Empty/0 = current tariff from the input_number (or 0).
data_description.initial_savings: Optional. Added on top of the calculated starting credit.

error.same_sensor: The sensors must be different (export must not be among the production sensors).

init.title: Adjust amortization
init.description: Sensors and starting credit stay unchanged; only the live values here (cost, price, tariff).

data_description.investment_entity: input_number/sensor with the purchase cost in €.
data_description.price_entity: input_number/sensor with the grid price in €/kWh.
data_description.feed_in_entity: input_number/sensor with the tariff in €/kWh. Empty = 0.
